title: rhinanthus minor subsp. groenlandicus
intro: Rhinanthus minor subsp. groenlandicus, also known as the northern yellow-rattle or yellow-rattle, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae). This hemiparasitic plant is known to have medicinal properties, including the ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, due to its high content of iridoid glycosides. The health benefits of Rhinanthus minor subsp. groenlandicus include its potential to aid in weight loss, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
